body:
## Why this matters

The new homelab epic stack needs a **first concrete substrate lane** that higher-level storage and service validation can target. Without a repo-owned KubeVirt-backed homelab-role VM path, every later homelab issue will blur substrate work into application testing.

## Scope

Define the first VM-backed homelab substrate lane under #54:

- identify the first homelab-role VM shape this repo should own for follow-on validation
- define how that VM is provisioned within the lab's Argo + KubeVirt + GitOps model
- capture the minimum lifecycle evidence the lane must emit: provision, boot, reachability, reboot, teardown
- make the client-vs-cluster boundary explicit so this lane does not accidentally turn contributor systems into cluster nodes

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A concrete homelab-role VM lane is defined with the repo-owned workflow surface it should use
- [ ] The provisioning/lifecycle evidence required from that lane is documented clearly enough to implement without re-planning the substrate
- [ ] The issue states what belongs in substrate validation versus storage/service follow-up work
- [ ] The client-vs-cluster boundary is explicit and aligned with the parent epic

## Links

- Child of #54
- Source ideas: `projectbluefin/bluespeed#1`, `projectbluefin/bluespeed#26`, `projectbluefin/bluespeed#32`
title: feat: define homelab-role VM validation lane on KubeVirt
